title: The Universal Section Schema
summary: Every page, sidebar, infobox, and article body in this wiki compiles down to a single data shape: the UniversalSection.

content.body: Core data shape for everything the platform renders.

highlights.subtitle: Buckets
highlights.description: subject, content, media, links, display, spatial

content.body: The platform ships a registry of renderers keyed by display.layout or section type. A hero, a stats grid, a timeline, an article body, an infobox — all of them are UniversalSection objects on the wire, and all of them are plugged into the registry at runtime. Wiki-specific renderers are additive plugins; they never fork the core.

paragraphs.subtitle: subject
paragraphs.description: Title, subtitle, category, ordering, tags. The identity of the thing.

paragraphs.subtitle: content
paragraphs.description: Body text, paragraphs, highlights, stats, items — the prose.

paragraphs.subtitle: media
paragraphs.description: Images, themed images, background plates, video, 3D model references.

paragraphs.subtitle: display
paragraphs.description: Layout key, text width, alignment, animation, feature flags, access roles.

content.body: New fields are always optional and always additive. An old app can read a new section and simply ignore the extra keys; a new app can render an old section because every bucket is optional except id and type. The schema versions itself forward without breaking anything downstream.
